title: polypodium pleopeltis
intro: Polypodium pleopeltis, also known as resurrection fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the southeastern United States, particularly in the subtropical regions of Florida and Georgia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Polypodium pleopeltis include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Polypodium pleopeltis is often used in teas, tinctures, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
